creator: tomek.cedro@gmail.com
text:
Hello guys! :-)

The problem I have reported was about binary urjtag using libftdi
0.14, when 0.17 is already out. Also there are some problems with
building the port. Below is the output. I am using the fresh ports
tree.

When trying to pkg_add the binary package it complains that another
version of the libftdi is required, also it won't run because of the
library mismatch. If it is possible please update the binary according
to the libftdi version.

# pkg_add -r urjtag
Fetching ftp://ftp.freebsd.org/pub/FreeBSD/ports/i386/packages-8.0-release/Latest/urjtag.tbz...
Done.
pkg_add: warning: package 'urjtag-0.10' requires 'libftdi-0.14_1', but
'libftdi-0.17' is installed
# jtag
/libexec/ld-elf.so.1: Shared object "libftdi.so.15" not found,
required by "jtag"


When I tried to build the urjtag from the ports, still, it gives me
the build error:

(...)
gmake[3]: Leaving directory `/usr/ports/devel/urjtag/work/urjtag-0.10/src/bsdl'
gmake[3]: Entering directory `/usr/ports/devel/urjtag/work/urjtag-0.10/src'
cc -DHAVE_CONFIG_H -I. -I.. -DLOCALEDIR=\"/usr/local/share/locale\"
-I.. -I../include -I/usr/local/include -I/usr/local/include  -O2 -pipe
--std=c99 -fno-strict-aliasing -Wall -MT jtag.o -MD -MP -MF
.deps/jtag.Tpo -c -o jtag.o jtag.c
mv -f .deps/jtag.Tpo .deps/jtag.Po
cc  -O2 -pipe --std=c99 -fno-strict-aliasing -Wall  -L/usr/local/lib
-o jtag jtag.o -Lcmd -lcmd -Ltap -ltap -Lpart -lpart -Llib -ljtaglib
-Lflash -lflash -Ltap -ltap -Lflash -lflash -Lbus -lbus -lm
/usr/local/lib/libintl.so /usr/local/lib/libiconv.so -Wl,-rpath
-Wl,/usr/local/lib -Lsvf -lsvf -Lbsdl -lbsdl   -L/usr/local/lib -lftdi
-lusb  -lreadline
gmake[3]: Leaving directory `/usr/ports/devel/urjtag/work/urjtag-0.10/src'
gmake[2]: Leaving directory `/usr/ports/devel/urjtag/work/urjtag-0.10/src'
gmake[1]: Leaving directory `/usr/ports/devel/urjtag/work/urjtag-0.10'
*** Error code 1

Stop in /usr/ports/devel/urjtag.
*** Error code 1

Stop in /usr/ports/devel/urjtag.

It could be bsdl related (there was a workaround with bsdl disabled
but I dont remember exactly right now). It is also worth mentioning
that libusb is not found so there is a big chance that USB JTAG cables
not based on FTDI will not work! Right now I have only two FTDI based
devices, but I have ordered the Xilinx and Altera platform cables, so
this will be verified soon :-)

(...)
checking for libusb-config... no
checking for usb_find_devices... no
(...)
jtag is now configured for

  Detected libusb      : no
  Detected libftdi     : yes
  Detected libftd2xx   : no
  Detected inpout32    : no
  Build SVF player     : yes
  Build BSDL subsystem : yes
  Bus drivers          : au1500 avr32 bcm1250 bf526_ezkit bf527_ezkit
bf533_stamp bf533_ezkit bf537_stamp bf537_ezkit bf538f_ezkit
bf548_ezkit bf561_ezkit bscoach ejtag ejtag_dma fjmem ixp425 jopcyc
h7202 lh7a400 mpc5200 mpc824x ppc405ep ppc440gx_ebc8 prototype pxa2x0
pxa27x s3c4510 sa1110 sh7727 sh7750r sh7751r sharc_21065L slsup3
tx4925 zefant_xs3
  Cable drivers        : arcom byteblaster dlc5 ea253 ei012 ft2232
igloo keithkoep lattice mpcbdm triton usbblaster wiggler
  Lowlevel drivers     : direct ftdi ppi


Best regards,
Tomek
